candidates.reasoning: The text 'الاتحاد الدولي للبنوك' (Union Internationale de Banques) refers to UIB, a major bank operating specifically in Tunisia. The combination of Arabic and French (Latin script) is characteristic of Tunisia's linguistic landscape.

The combination of Mediterranean vegetation, white-washed architecture with flat roofs, and the specific arid-yet-coastal light is highly characteristic of Tunisia. The settlement pattern of low-rise white buildings along a paved road is typical for Tunisian urban areas.
Algeria shares very similar Mediterranean coastal geography, soil types, and white-washed architectural styles with Tunisia. The landscape is nearly indistinguishable from eastern Algeria.

The arid climate and white-washed building style are common across the Maghreb region, including Morocco, though the specific urban layout here is slightly more reminiscent of the central/eastern Maghreb.

Road markings are minimal on this urban street, which is common in the region.

The presence of a yellow taxi (specifically a Fiat Punto/similar small hatchback) is very common in Tunisia. The white-washed building architecture with flat roofs and the specific style of the black decorative metal fencing on the right are highly characteristic of Tunisian urban areas.
